name: Ramy Youssef
bio: Ramy Youssef is an American stand-up comedian, actor, and writer raised in New Jersey by Egyptian immigrant parents, whose work explores faith, identity, and life as a millennial Muslim-American. He created and stars in the Hulu series Ramy, winning a Golden Globe and a Peabody, and released the HBO specials Feelings and More Feelings. He also co-created Mo Amer's Netflix series Mo and the animated comedy #1 Happy Family USA, and appeared in the film Poor Things.

timeline.ev: Begins his stand-up and performing career
Performs stand-up on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert
Series Ramy debuts on Hulu; releases HBO special Feelings
Wins Golden Globe for Best Actor and a Peabody Award
Releases HBO special More Feelings; hosts Saturday Night Live
Co-creates the animated series #1 Happy Family USA

accolades: Golden Globe, Best Actor in a Television Series Musical or Comedy (2020)
Peabody Award for the Netflix series Mo (2023)
Two Primetime Emmy nominations for Ramy

bits.t: Why Do They Make Her Wear That
